label: security_sql_injection
summary: Reject on SQL injection. The handler passes `req.query.q` verbatim into a raw SQL string via `$queryRawUnsafe` — every search request is an injection point. This is not a cosmetic fix: the implementation approach must change to parameterized queries before any other concern matters. Secondary blockers: route registration is missing from the diff (criterion 1 unverifiable), and the performance claim rests on an assumed GIN trigram index with no migration evidence (criterion 4 unverifiable). The logical direction — ILIKE, 100-row limit, early-exit on empty q — is sound. Reimplement with `prisma.$queryRaw` tagged templates or Prisma ORM `mode: 'insensitive'`, add the router wiring, and include the migration or index evidence before re-review.

findings.description: `$queryRawUnsafe` interpolates `req.query.q` directly into SQL — attacker controls arbitrary SQL via the q parameter (classic injection: `' OR '1'='1`, stacked statements, exfil via UNION).
findings.suggestion: Replace with Prisma tagged template `prisma.$queryRaw\`SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE name ILIKE ${'%' + q + '%'} LIMIT 100\`` or use `prisma.user.findMany({ where: { name: { contains: q, mode: 'insensitive' } }, take: 100 })`.

findings.description: Route registration for GET /api/users/search is absent from the diff — `searchUsers` may be an unreachable dead function.
findings.suggestion: Include the router file change that wires `GET /api/users/search` to `searchUsers`, or show it already existed.

findings.description: ILIKE with a leading wildcard (`'%q%'`) cannot use a B-tree index; requires a `pg_trgm` GIN index. Executor asserts this index exists per prior migration, but no migration file appears in the diff.
findings.suggestion: Include the migration that adds the GIN trigram index, or run `\d users` and paste the index list as evidence. Without it, criterion 4 is unverifiable and the query will full-scan at 1M rows.

findings.description: Test file is listed in `filesChanged` but is absent from the diff — cannot verify coverage of SQL metacharacter input, mixed-case queries, empty result, or the no-q early-return path.
findings.suggestion: Include the test diff. At minimum verify: `q` containing `'`, `%`, `_`; mixed-case search term vs lowercase stored name; empty array returned when no match; response when q is omitted.

fullReview.summary: Reject on SQL injection. The handler passes `req.query.q` verbatim into a raw SQL string via `$queryRawUnsafe` — every search request is an injection point. This is not a cosmetic fix: the implementation approach must change to parameterized queries before any other concern matters. Secondary blockers: route registration is missing from the diff (criterion 1 unverifiable), and the performance claim rests on an assumed GIN trigram index with no migration evidence (criterion 4 unverifiable). The logical direction — ILIKE, 100-row limit, early-exit on empty q — is sound. Reimplement with `prisma.$queryRaw` tagged templates or Prisma ORM `mode: 'insensitive'`, add the router wiring, and include the migration or index evidence before re-review.

criteriaAssessment.evidence: Handler reads `req.query.q` correctly, but no route registration appears in the diff. Cannot confirm `GET /api/users/search` is reachable.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Returns users whose name contains the search term (case-insensitive)
criteriaAssessment.evidence: ILIKE `'%q%'` is logically correct for case-insensitive partial match, but the query is SQL-injectable and cannot be shipped. Functional intent is present; implementation is broken at the construction layer.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Returns empty array if no matches
criteriaAssessment.evidence: Prisma raw query returns an empty array when no rows match; no special handling is needed or wrong here.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Performance acceptable for tables up to 1M rows (use index)
criteriaAssessment.evidence: Executor assumes a GIN trigram index exists per prior migration, but no migration file is in the diff. Leading-wildcard ILIKE without `pg_trgm` GIN = full sequential scan at 1M rows.
